item.text:
I couldn’t apply the change because the session filesystem is read-only and approvals are disabled. Baseline tests currently pass: `python3 -m unittest tests.test_rate_limiter`.

Apply this patch:

```diff
diff --git a/demo/rate_limiter.py b/demo/rate_limiter.py
@@
     def allow(self) -> bool:
         now = time.time()
         self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
         if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
             self.calls.append(now)
             return True
         return False
+
+    def reset(self) -> None:
+        self.calls.clear()
diff --git a/tests/test_rate_limiter.py b/tests/test_rate_limiter.py
@@
     def test_rejects_within_window_and_prunes_expired_calls(self):
         limiter = RateLimiter(limit=2, window=10)
@@
 
         self.assertEqual(limiter.calls, [11])
+
+    def test_reset_clears_calls_and_allows_again(self):
+        limiter = RateLimiter(limit=1, window=10)
+
+        with patch("demo.rate_limiter.time.time", side_effect=[0, 1, 2]):
+            self.assertTrue(limiter.allow())
+            self.assertFalse(limiter.allow())
+            limiter.reset()
+            self.assertEqual(limiter.calls, [])
+            self.assertTrue(limiter.allow())
+
+        self.assertEqual(limiter.calls, [2])
```

The test confirms `reset()` clears stored calls and that `allow()` behavior remains unchanged by exercising the normal `allow()` path after reset.
